summary: LocalOrders Phase 1 backend (FastAPI + MongoDB) tested end-to-end via the public ingress URL. 26/26 pytest cases pass covering OTP send (success/duplicate/invalid), buyer + seller registration (mock OTP=123456, PIN match, PIN format, duplicate mobile), seller code auto-increment starting at SELLER-1001, login flow with remaining-attempts counter and 5-strike 15-min lockout (HTTP 423), /auth/me bearer-token enforcement, /users/me role-aware update (sellers can set businessName, buyers get 400), seller inventory CRUD with soft delete and include_inactive, unitType whitelist (all 10 allowed, anything else 422), buyer 403 on /seller/*, and zero Mongo _id leakage in any response. No critical or minor issues found.

minor.issue: Returns the mock OTP value ('123456') in the response body. Intentional for Phase 1 (documented in PRD), but in production this field must be removed.

Returns HTTP 401 'Missing token' when no Authorization header is sent. FastAPI/Starlette norm is 403 for missing creds, 401 for invalid creds; either is acceptable. Tests assert 401 (matches current code).

action_items: Phase 2: remove `mockOtp` field from /api/auth/otp/send response once real Firebase OTP is wired.
Consider returning a structured body for 423 lockout (e.g. {detail, retryAfterSeconds}) so the mobile UI can render a precise countdown instead of parsing the message string.
Optional: 404 on /api/auth/login for unknown mobile leaks account existence. For privacy parity with real auth, consider returning a generic 401 'Invalid credentials'.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 428 lines — fine for now, but split into routers/auth.py + routers/inventory.py + models.py when Phase 2 lands.
next_seller_code() does two find_one_and_update calls when seq<1001; safe but slightly racy under heavy concurrency. Acceptable for Phase 1.
JWT_SECRET defaults to a literal in source if env not set — fine for dev, must be enforced via env in prod.
All Mongo reads correctly project away _id (or use the user_public/item_public mappers); verified no _id in any response.
PIN bcrypt-hashed; pinHash never returned in user_public — good.
seed_data_creation: No persistent seeds. Each test class self-registers fresh accounts using random 10-digit mobiles starting with 9.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_localorders_api.py; uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL. Random mobiles avoid duplicate-mobile 409 across runs. Lockout test fully exhausts an account — re-running will create a new locked account each time (acceptable, isolated per test).
